primary_legislation.england_wales: Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations 1992 (as amended 2002)
primary_legislation.scotland: Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations 1992 (as amended 2002)
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Health and Safety (Display Screen Equipment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992

notes: Steps are identical to school MOD-024. GP practices typically have a high proportion of defined DSE users: GPs, nurses, admin staff, and dispensers all use screens for the majority of their working day. Branch surgeries must also be included in the assessment.

logbooks.title: Annual DSE Self-Assessment Review
logbooks.frequency_detail: At minimum annually. Also review if a DSE user changes workstation, changes role, or reports discomfort.

steps.description: Identify all 'defined users' — staff who use display screen equipment as a significant part of their normal work (typically 1 hour or more continuously per day). In a GP practice this includes: GPs (clinical system, electronic prescribing), practice nurses (clinical system), reception and admin staff, dispensers (dispensing practices).

Issue or make available a DSE self-assessment checklist to each defined user — covering: screen position and height, keyboard and mouse position, chair adjustment, lighting and glare, posture awareness.
Each defined user completes the self-assessment, notes any issues and returns it to the Practice Manager or H&S lead.

Review completed assessments — arrange any adjustments needed (chair adjustment, monitor riser, footrest, wrist rest, alternative keyboard/mouse). For clinical staff using laptops during home visits, a separate portable device assessment may be needed.
Remind defined users of their entitlement to an eyesight test at the employer's expense (the practice must pay for a standard eye test for any defined DSE user who requests one).
Record: date, list of defined users, assessment completed (yes/no), issues identified, adjustments made, eye test referrals issued, reviewer name.
